generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR providing spatial perception for real-time environmental mapping with 2D/3D point cloud data generation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance DepthCameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i depth camera providing depth perception for vehicle surroundings and object detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor suite providing vehicle state data including speed, position, orientation, and motor/servo feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Feedback sensors monitoring motor torque, voltage commands, and mechanical responses for closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Precision servo feedback system tracking steering angle, position, and dynamic adjustments for path correction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw and processed LiDAR point cloud data transmitted at high frequency (≥10Hz) for object tracking and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance DepthCameraDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Depth camera data providing 3D spatial information for object detection and environmental mapping."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCameraSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Continuously streamed vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorActuationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor actuation feedback including torque, voltage commands, and mechanical response validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance SteeringServoDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering servo feedback for dynamic path adjustments and closed-loop control validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink-based model for LiDAR point cloud clustering and tracking to identify objects and vehicle surroundings."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler, SimulinkClusteringEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateEstimationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter or machine learning model for vehicle trajectory and state estimation using sensor fusion."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2StateEstimationEnabler, JetsonEdgeProcessingEnabler
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithm embedded in Digital Twin Entity for real-time desired speed computation based on lead vehicle dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ControlEnabler, JetsonEdgeProcessingEnabler
]

instance BatteryStateOfChargeModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based or data-driven model for battery SoC prediction and degradation forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkBatteryModelingEnabler, JetsonEdgeProcessingEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured and pre-processed LiDAR point cloud data after clustering and noise filtering for object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringModel, ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed depth camera data integrated with LiDAR for enhanced 3D environmental mapping."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and validated vehicle state data for real-time monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateEstimationModel, ROS2StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed motor actuation feedback for closed-loop control validation and safety monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ControlEnabler
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed steering servo feedback for dynamic path adjustments and control validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ControlEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2-based software platform for LiDAR point cloud processing, clustering, and tracking using ROS2 topics."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringService, ObjectTrackingService
]

instance ROS2StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2-based state estimation framework integrating sensor fusion and Kalman filters for vehicle trajectory prediction."
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateMonitoringService
]

instance ROS2ControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2-based control system for adaptive cruise control and closed-loop actuation using voltage commands."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveCruiseControlService, DynamicPathAdjustmentService
]

instance SimulinkClusteringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink-based enabler for point cloud clustering and tracking in autonomous vehicle perception systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringService
]

instance SimulinkBatteryModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink-based battery system modeling for SoC prediction and thermal management in electric vehicles."
    DTDFVocab:enables BatteryStateMonitoringService
]

instance JetsonEdgeProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson edge AI platform for real-time processing of sensor data with low-latency communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveCruiseControlService, BatteryStateMonitoringService, VehicleStateMonitoringService
]

instance WiFi6CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi 6/6E protocol for low-latency communication between physical and digital twin entities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance SQLiteEdgeDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge database for local caching of sensor logs and historical models in autonomous systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStorageService
]

instance ISO23247CompliantArchitectureEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247 Reference Architecture for entity-based digital twin system design in automotive and industrial IoT."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemDesignService
]

// C6: Services
instance PointCloudClusteringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks and projected driving paths."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectDetectionInsight, DrivingPathInsight
]

instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time object tracking and dynamic environmental mapping using fused LiDAR and depth camera data."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalMappingInsight, ObstacleDetectionInsight
]

instance VehicleStateMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of vehicle speed, position, and orientation for real-time operational awareness."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, TrajectoryInsight
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control system dynamically adjusting vehicle speed based on lead vehicle dynamics and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalSpeedInsight, CruiseControlAction
]

instance DynamicPathAdjustmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time path adjustment service using steering servo feedback and environmental data for collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:provides PathAdjustmentInsight, SteeringAction
]

instance BatteryStateMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitoring battery state-of-charge and health using physics-based and data-driven models."
    DTDFVocab:provides BatterySoCInsight, DegradationForecastInsight
]

instance DegradationForecastingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive service forecasting battery degradation over time using historical data and operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides DegradationForecastInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "High-frequency data transmission between physical and digital twin entities using Wi-Fi 6/6E."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTransmissionInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring dashboard for operational data visualization and control."
    DTDFVocab:provides MonitoringDashboardInsight, ControlInterfaceAction
]

instance SystemDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for designing and implementing ISO 23247 compliant digital twin architectures."
    DTDFVocab:provides InteroperabilityInsight
]

instance DataStorageService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for storing and retrieving sensor logs and historical data in edge databases."
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight
]

instance InteroperabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring compliance with ISO 23247 standards for system interoperability."
    DTDFVocab:provides InteroperabilityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ObjectDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified objects and their spatial relationships in the vehicle's surroundings from LiDAR point cloud data."
]

instance DrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected driving path based on clustered objects and vehicle state for path planning."
]

instance EnvironmentalMappingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D environmental map combining LiDAR and depth camera data for situational awareness."
]

instance ObstacleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected obstacles and potential collision risks in the vehicle's path."
]

instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current vehicle state including speed, position, and orientation for real-time monitoring."
]

instance TrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted vehicle trajectory based on current state and environmental conditions."
]

instance OptimalSpeedInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computed optimal desired speed for adaptive cruise control based on lead vehicle dynamics."
]

instance CruiseControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of vehicle speed to maintain safe distance from lead vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PathAdjustmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommended path adjustments based on obstacle detection and environmental mapping."
]

instance SteeringAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated steering adjustments to avoid obstacles and follow optimal path."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance BatterySoCInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current state-of-charge and health status of the vehicle's battery system."
]

instance DegradationForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted battery degradation over time based on operational conditions and historical data."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommended maintenance actions based on battery degradation forecasts."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataTransmissionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Status of data transmission between physical and digital twin entities with latency metrics."
]

instance MonitoringDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of operational data including vehicle state, sensor inputs, and system health."
]

instance ControlInterfaceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User interface for monitoring and controlling cruise parameters and system operations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical sensor logs and model data for analysis and trend identification."
]

instance InteroperabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Compliance status with ISO 23247 standards for system interoperability."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller regulating motor speed via voltage commands for closed-loop control."
]

instance VehicleMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric motor directly actuating mechanical motion interfaced with the speed controller."
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Precision servo executing steering commands for dynamic path adjustments."
]

// C1: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Automotive environment for autonomous vehicles including roads, traffic, and dynamic obstacles."
    base:contains LiDARPointCloudSensor, DepthCameraSensor, VehicleStateSensor,
                MotorActuationSensor, SteeringServoSensor,
                ElectronicSpeedController, VehicleMotor, SteeringServo
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Centralized digital representation of the physical vehicle including models, data, and control mechanisms."
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringModel, VehicleStateEstimationModel,
                AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, BatteryStateOfChargeModel,
                ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler, ROS2StateEstimationEnabler,
                ROS2ControlEnabler, SimulinkClusteringEnabler,
                SimulinkBatteryModelingEnabler, JetsonEdgeProcessingEnabler
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User-facing interface for monitoring operational data and controlling system parameters."
    base:contains MonitoringDashboardInsight, ControlInterfaceAction
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) is governed by control flows originating from the Digital Twin Entity and targeting the Physical Twin Entity, primarily through the following mechanisms:

1. **Command Types and Control Flows**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Generated by the *Actuation FE* to control physical components such as motors, steering servos, and adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems. These commands are derived from processed data (e.g., object tracks, projected paths) and user inputs.
   - **Controlling FE Commands**: The *Controlling FE* (e.g., ACC logic) issues control signals to adjust vehicle behavior (e.g., acceleration/deceleration, steering corrections) based on real-time Digital Twin analysis.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - Commands are formatted and translated via the *Data Translation* component to ensure compatibility with physical actuators. Specific formats (e.g., ROS2 message types, UDP packets) are not detailed but are implied to align with the system’s communication infrastructure.

3. **Validation and Authentication**:
   - The document does not specify explicit validation or authentication mechanisms for control commands. However, the *Controlling FE* and *Actuation FE* imply a structured hierarchy where commands are vetted before execution (e.g., safety thresholds may be inherent in ACC logic).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - Physical actuators (e.g., motors, servos) provide feedback to the Digital Twin via the *Actuation FE*, closing the control loop. This feedback includes confirmation of command execution (e.g., achieved steering angle, motor RPM) and real-time state updates.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - Not explicitly described in the document. Potential conflicts (e.g., competing commands from user and ACC) may be resolved via priority rules embedded in the *Controlling FE* or *Actuation FE*, though specifics are absent.

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - The *Actuation FE* likely generates acknowledgments or status updates upon successful command execution, which are propagated back to the Digital Twin for monitoring and logging.

**Key Functional Elements Involved**:
- *Controlling FE* (e.g., ACC logic, user input processing)
- *Actuation FE* (motor/servo control, command execution)
- *Data Translation* (command formatting and translation)
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by standards but not implemented here. The system does not explicitly specify varying time scales for different services, but the **ROS2-based architecture** (with individual ROS2 packages per Functional Entity, FE) implies real-time or near-real-time processing constraints for sensor-actuator loops, data pre-processing, and device communication within the **Physical Twin Entity**. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **100 ms synchronization window**, ensuring deterministic behavior for time-sensitive operations like actuation and point cloud clustering/tracking. No explicit mention of asynchronous or event-driven temporal patterns is provided."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its components using a **hybrid architecture** rooted in the **ISO 23247 RA**, where **Functional Entities (FEs)** are mapped to hardware components and implemented as **ROS2 packages**. Key deviations include the **Physical Twin Entity’s expanded role** (beyond OME’s resource-specific scope) and the **splitting of the User Interface FE** into visualization and control subcomponents. Inter-entity communication occurs over a **Wi-Fi network**, with no explicit mention of middleware-specific coordination (e.g., ROS2 topics/services or QoS policies). Resource management is implicit via the **entity-based decomposition**, where each hardware component hosts a dedicated entity, but no centralized orchestrator or dynamic resource allocation mechanisms are described. The system prioritizes **deterministic latency** (≤100 ms) over adaptive orchestration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT system must adhere to **real-time and high-fidelity** requirements, as digital twins are defined as computational reflections of physical assets with minimal latency. The **ISO 23247 framework** emphasizes modularity, suggesting **validation and verification** can be performed at the **Functional Entity (FE)** level. However, the document specifies **quantitative fidelity constraints** for the case study, including:
  - **Network latency**: ≤100 ms from the vehicle to the DT Entity (Requirement R7).
  - **Clock synchronization**: Components must synchronize to an accuracy of ≤100 ms (Requirement R8).
  The standard does not prescribe **uncertainty quantification methods**, **error detection/correction mechanisms**, or **quality assurance processes**, leaving these as implementation-specific considerations. The **entity-based architecture** facilitates modular testing but does not enforce standardized validation protocols."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** infrastructure is defined by the following technical specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **ROS2 Network**: Internal software component communication occurs within a **ROS2** network, enabling modular, real-time data exchange between functional elements (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, *Data Translation*).
   - **Wi-Fi + UDP**: Inter-entity communication (Physical Twin → Digital Twin and vice versa) uses **Wi-Fi** at the network layer with **UDP** at the transport layer (as partially described in the document).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical**: Data flows from Physical Twin sensors → Digital Twin software components → User Interface, with clear separation between internal (ROS2) and external (Wi-Fi/UDP) communication layers.
   - **Decentralized**: Components communicate peer-to-peer within ROS2, while Wi-Fi/UDP handles broader entity-level data exchange.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Latency**: The document mentions a **100 ms** latency requirement (R7) for real-time monitoring and control, though specific bandwidth values (e.g., Mbps) are not provided.
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for applications like ACC, where timely data processing and command execution are essential.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the document. Reliability may be inherent in ROS2’s Quality of Service (QoS) mechanisms or Wi-Fi’s inherent redundancy, but specifics are absent.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - Not described in the provided documents. Security measures (e.g., encryption, access control) are not mentioned for either ROS2 or Wi-Fi/UDP communication.

**Key Technologies**:
- **ROS2**: For internal software communication.
- **Wi-Fi + UDP**: For inter-entity data transmission.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is characterized by the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: The Physical Twin Entity (e.g., vehicle sensors, actuators) hosts lightweight components (e.g., *Data Pre-Processing FE*, *Actuation FE*) locally to minimize latency.
   - **Digital Twin Entity**: Likely deployed on a **workstation or server** external to the vehicle (as implied by the need for 3D visualization and user interfaces). Specific platforms (e.g., cloud, on-premise) are not detailed.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Physical Twin (Vehicle)**:
     - **Hardware**: Includes sensors (LiDAR, cameras) and embedded systems (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson for preprocessing).
     - **Computational**: Real-time processing of point clouds and vehicle state data.
   - **Digital Twin (Workstation/Server)**:
     - **Storage**: Data is stored in an **SQL database** (as inferred from Table I’s *Data Storage* entry under *Reporting FE*).
     - **Computational**: Supports visualization (e.g., 3D rendering of vehicle and environment), data translation, and user interface operations.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - Not explicitly described. Scalability may be achieved through modular ROS2 components or distributed storage, but specifics are absent.

4. **Deployment Models**:
   - **Hybrid**: Combines edge (vehicle) and centralized (workstation/server) deployment. The *Data Translation* component acts as a bridge between these layers.
   - **External Storage**: Data is stored external to the vehicle (e.g., for reporting, analysis, or long-term retention).

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - Not detailed in the document. Reliability may depend on redundant sensor inputs, ROS2’s fault-tolerant mechanisms, or Wi-Fi connectivity, but no explicit assurances are provided.

**Key Components**:
- **NVIDIA Jetson**: Used on the physical side for preprocessing.
- **SQL Database**: For structured data storage on the Digital Twin side.
- **ROS2 Network**: Enables modular and scalable deployment of software components.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard (2021)**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - An **entity-based architecture** with standardized roles for **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection/Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**.
  - **Interoperability standards** are implied via modular **Functional Entities (FEs)**, though no specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MTConnect) are mandated—only **OPC Unified Architecture** is mentioned as a *possible* implementation choice.
  - **Compliance frameworks** are not detailed beyond ISO 23247, and **certification requirements** are not referenced. The standard emphasizes **information exchange** (Part 4) but does not specify **validation or certification bodies**. **Industry-specific standards** (e.g., automotive, aerospace) are not addressed in the provided context."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems**, specifying performance requirements and test procedures to ensure safe operation. Key safety considerations include **network latency constraints**, where the **Digital Twin System (DTS)** must maintain a **maximum latency of 100 ms** (as per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 Release 15**). The system must also comply with **functional safety standards** as referenced in **ISO 26262-1:2018**, though explicit implementation details (e.g., ASIL levels, fail-safe mechanisms) are not provided in the documents. No explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols, access control, authentication, or threat mitigation** is found in the provided references. The **Dalibor et al. (2020)** paper discusses **model-driven architecture for interactive digital twin cockpits** but does not specify **HIL/SIL testing methodologies** or **safety-critical system considerations** beyond the broader context of digital twin integration."
]
